Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 12:33 ET
Massive SPY put buying dominates as dealers amplify downside into NFP
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. SPY's $-2.9B negative gamma creates dangerous amplification ahead of tomorrow's NFP print, with 600k put contracts bought at 654-655 strikes.
  • 2. Index-single name P/C divergence (1.37 vs 0.92) signals institutional dispersion hedging into jobs data.
  • 3. Nike's explosive two-way flow (+123% calls, +109% puts) ahead of earnings creates textbook straddle opportunity.
NARRATIVE — negative gamma amplification

Dealers sit in maximum amplification mode with SPY's $-2.9B negative gamma — the largest short positioning since August's unwind — creating treacherous conditions into tomorrow's NFP release. The flow confirms this anxiety with over 600,000 SPY put contracts concentrated at 654-655 strikes, representing $40B+ in notional hedging demand just 1% below current levels. QQQ's contrasting +$1.1B positive gamma suggests tech-specific resilience, but won't contain broader market volatility when SPY dealers are forced to sell into declines. The P/C divergence tells the institutional story: index puts at 1.37 versus single-name calls at 0.92 represents classic dispersion positioning ahead of binary data. Sophisticated money is hedging systematic risk while maintaining single-name upside exposure, expecting stock-specific performance to diverge from index weakness. This setup intensifies with implied correlation at 0.18 — historically low levels that reward dispersion trades when volatility spikes. Nike dominates single-name flow with explosive two-way volume (+123% calls, +109% puts) creating a $4+ straddle ahead of Thursday earnings. The simultaneous demand for both wings signals expectations for a >8% move in either direction, with options pricing in maximum uncertainty. Energy and financials also see significant institutional call accumulation through XLE and XLF block trades, suggesting sector rotation bets into the jobs print.

Layer 1: VIX 23.9Layer 2: SPY GEX -$2.9BLayer 3: Index P/C 1.37

GREEK PRESSURE KEY
